summary:
The auriculotemporal nerve provides sensory innervation to the temporalis muscle and articular structures within the capsule of the TMJ. This makes it a critical component for assessing pain or other sensations related to these areas during diagnostic evaluations. \boxed{A}

---

**Final Summary:**  
Sensations from the capsule of the TMJ are primarily supplied by the auriculotemporal nerve; thus, understanding its role aids in diagnosing conditions affecting jaw function and associated pains. 

This summary captures only two main aspects while focusing directly on what was asked regarding which specific nerves provide sensation to parts inside the TMJ’s capsule. It also ties back into practical implications such as diagnosis. \boxed{} To align with your request more precisely without adding extra content beyond necessary details,

Key Points Summed Up In Two Sentences For Question Relevance Are As Follows,
- Auriculotemporal Nerve Supplies Capsule Of Temporomandibular Joint
- Important For Assessments Related Pain Or Conditions Affecting Jaw Function
